label: medical_distress_trauma_run_1_dispatch
qwen: photograph of two paramedics in dark navy uniforms sitting in the cab of a parked ambulance at night, one talking into a radio handset with focused expression, the other already starting the engine, lit dashboard glowing on their faces, rain on the windshield, dispatch radio chatter active
video: the paramedic sets down the handset and the driver immediately throws on the emergency lights, audio is dispatch radio: car ten respond code three to mercy general, that's a possible cardiac, the engine starting, sirens beginning to wail

label: medical_distress_trauma_run_2_ambulance_race
video: the ambulance races down rain-slicked nighttime city streets at speed, lights strobing red and blue across wet pavement and storefronts, traffic pulling aside as it passes, audio is the full siren wailing, tires hissing on wet road, dispatch radio crackling updates, windshield wipers slapping at high speed

label: medical_distress_trauma_run_3_hospital_arrival
video: the paramedics rush a fully blanketed patient on a wheeled gurney through the bright sliding doors of a hospital emergency entrance, medical equipment rattling, IV pole swaying, scrub-wearing trauma team waiting just inside, audio is rapid heart-monitor beeping, hurried footsteps on linoleum, doors hissing open, urgent shouted medical jargon, intercom paging Doctor Chen to trauma room three

label: medical_distress_trauma_run_4_trauma_team_prep
video: the trauma team in green scrubs and masks moves with practiced choreography around the trauma bay, prepping IV lines, calling out vitals, hanging fluids, the patient remains fully blanketed, no graphic content shown, audio is the team's overlapping calm-but-urgent voices: BP ninety palp, get me two large bore IVs, type and cross four units, monitor beeping steady

label: medical_distress_trauma_run_5_recovery_stable
video: the camera pulls back from a peaceful recovery room where the patient is now resting under clean white sheets, a single nurse adjusting a smooth steady IV drip, sunlight streaming through the window, the heart monitor showing a strong steady rhythm, audio is the slow steady beep of the monitor, soft footsteps, distant hospital ambience, a faint exhale from the nurse, then a single long held cello note of resolution
